message:
fix(demo_showcase): resolve clippy errors in Backend impl

- Add #[allow(clippy::large_enum_variant)] to Backend enum
- Add #[allow(clippy::missing_errors_doc, clippy::must_use_candidate)] to Backend impl
- Add #[allow(clippy::cast_possible_wrap, clippy::cast_sign_loss)] to register_hit_area
- Fix stats() return type to RenderStatsView<'_> (missing lifetime)
- Add #[allow(clippy::missing_const_for_fn, clippy::must_use_candidate)] to RenderStatsView impl

All quality gates pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -144,7 +144,7 @@ {"id":"bd-2x0.7","title":"Implement JSON tokenizer","description":"# Task: Implement JSON tokenizer ## Context JSON is ubiquitous for configuration and data exchange. It's also one of the simplest languages to tokenize, making it a good starting point and reference for testing the highlighting system. ## JSON Syntax (Complete Specification) JSON has exactly these token types: ### Structural Characters ``` { } [ ] : , ``` ### Literals ```json "string" // Strings (always double-quoted) 123 // Numbers (integer) -456 // Negative numbers 3.14 // Decimal numbers 1e10 // Scientific notation true // Boolean false // Boolean null // Null ``` ### String Escapes ```json \" \\ \/ \b \f \n \r \t \uXXXX ``` ## Implementation Create `src/highlight/languages/json.rs`: ```rust pub struct JsonTokenizer; impl Tokenizer for JsonTokenizer { fn name(&self) -> &str { "JSON" } fn extensions(&self) -> &[&str] { &["json", "jsonc", "json5"] } fn tokenize_line(&self, line: &str, state: LineState) -> (Vec, LineState) { // JSON is line-based for most purposes // Strings technically can't span lines in strict JSON // But we handle JSONC (with comments) and JSON5 (with multi-line) } } ``` ### Token Mapping | JSON Element | TokenKind | |-------------|-----------| | `"key":` (object key) | Identifier or String | | `"value"` | String | | `\n`, `\t`, etc. | StringEscape | | `123`, `-3.14` | Number | | `true`, `false` | Boolean | | `null` | Constant | | `{`, `}`, `[`, `]` | Punctuation | | `:`, `,` | Delimiter | | `//` comment (JSONC) | Comment | | `/* */` comment (JSONC) | CommentBlock | ## Edge Cases - Distinguishing object keys from string values (keys are followed by `:`) - Unicode escapes (`\uXXXX`) - Scientific notation (`1e-10`) - JSONC comment support - Invalid JSON (highlight as Error) ## Dependencies - Requires: bd-2x0.2 (Tokenizer trait) ## Testing ```rust #[test] fn test_json_strings() { ... } #[test] fn test_json_numbers() { ... } #[test] fn test_json_booleans_null() { ... } #[test] fn test_json_structure() { ... } #[test] fn test_json_escapes() { ... } #[test] fn test_jsonc_comments() { ... } ``` ## Files to Create/Modify - CREATE: `src/highlight/languages/json.rs` - MODIFY: `src/highlight/languages/mod.rs` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All JSON value types highlighted correctly - [ ] Object keys distinguished from string values - [ ] String escapes highlighted - [ ] Numbers with all formats (int, float, scientific, negative) - [ ] JSONC comment support - [ ] Invalid syntax highlighted as Error - [ ] Test coverage >90% (JSON is simple enough for high coverage)","notes":"## Performance Requirements Same as all tokenizers (per AGENTS.md): - **Zero allocations per token** - Use &str slices into source - **<1μs per line** for typical JSON - JSON is simpler than Rust, so performance should be even better ## Testing with Tracing Per bd-2x0.11: - All tests use tracing (info!, debug!) - Run: RUST_LOG=debug cargo test json_tokenizer -- --nocapture ## Note on JSONC Support JSONC (JSON with Comments) is common in VS Code configs. Support single-line `//` and block `/* */` comments, but mark the file extension separately (.jsonc) to not confuse strict JSON parsers.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:45:17.329963585Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T12:12:13.051156990Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T12:12:13.051138806Z","close_reason":"Implemented JSON tokenizer + tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["highlight","lang"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2x0.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2x0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:52:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2x0.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2x0.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:52:24Z","created_by":"import"}]} {"id":"bd-2x0.8","title":"Implement TOML tokenizer","description":"# Task: Implement TOML tokenizer ## Context TOML (Tom's Obvious Minimal Language) is the standard configuration format for Rust projects (Cargo.toml). Since OpenTUI is a Rust library, excellent TOML support is particularly relevant. ## TOML Syntax Elements ### Structure ```toml [section] [section.subsection] [[array.of.tables]] key = value "quoted key" = value dotted.key = value ``` ### Values ```toml # Strings string = "basic" multiline = """ multi line """ literal = 'no \escapes' literal_multiline = ''' multi line ''' # Numbers integer = 42 hex = 0xDEADBEEF octal = 0o755 binary = 0b11010110 float = 3.14 infinity = inf nan = nan # Boolean bool = true # Dates and times date = 2024-01-15 time = 14:30:00 datetime = 2024-01-15T14:30:00Z datetime_local = 2024-01-15T14:30:00 # Arrays array = [1, 2, 3] nested = [[1, 2], [3, 4]] # Inline tables inline = { key = "value", other = 42 } ``` ### Comments ```toml # Single line comment only key = "value" # End of line comment ``` ## Implementation Create `src/highlight/languages/toml.rs`: ```rust pub struct TomlTokenizer; impl Tokenizer for TomlTokenizer { fn name(&self) -> &str { "TOML" } fn extensions(&self) -> &[&str] { &["toml"] } fn tokenize_line(&self, line: &str, state: LineState) -> (Vec, LineState) { // Handle multi-line string continuation // Section headers [name] // Key = value pairs // Comments } } ``` ### Token Mapping | TOML Element | TokenKind | |-------------|-----------| | `[section]` | Type | | `[[array]]` | Type | | `key =` | Identifier | | `"string"` | String | | `42`, `0xFF` | Number | | `true`/`false` | Boolean | | `2024-01-15` | Number (or custom Date) | | `# comment` | Comment | | `=`, `,` | Operator | | `[`, `]`, `{`, `}` | Punctuation | ## Multi-line Handling - Multi-line basic strings: `""" ... """` - Multi-line literal strings: `''' ... '''` ## Edge Cases - Dotted keys (`package.version`) - Bare keys vs quoted keys - Date/time literals (many formats) - Unicode in strings and keys - Inline tables vs regular tables ## Dependencies - Requires: bd-2x0.2 (Tokenizer trait) ## Testing ```rust #[test] fn test_toml_sections() { ... } #[test] fn test_toml_key_values() { ... } #[test] fn test_toml_strings() { ... } #[test] fn test_toml_multiline_strings() { ... } #[test] fn test_toml_numbers() { ... } #[test] fn test_toml_dates() { ... } #[test] fn test_toml_arrays() { ... } #[test] fn test_toml_inline_tables() { ... } ``` ## Files to Create/Modify - CREATE: `src/highlight/languages/toml.rs` - MODIFY: `src/highlight/languages/mod.rs` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Section headers `[name]` highlighted as Type - [ ] Keys highlighted as Identifier - [ ] All string variants (basic, literal, multiline) - [ ] Numbers including special formats (hex, date/time) - [ ] Comments highlighted - [ ] Multi-line strings work correctly - [ ] Test coverage >85%","notes":"AGENTS.md alignment: Performance target <1μs/line. Zero-allocation tokenization - return spans not owned strings. Tests must use tracing (info!, debug!) for detailed logging. TOML is critical for Cargo.toml editing use cases.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:45:50.867576492Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T12:15:42.748398045Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T12:15:42.748379901Z","close_reason":"Implemented TOML tokenizer + tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["highlight","lang"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2x0.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2x0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:52:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2x0.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2x0.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:52:24Z","created_by":"import"}]} {"id":"bd-2x0.9","title":"Implement Markdown tokenizer","description":"# Task: Implement Markdown tokenizer ## Context Markdown is the de facto standard for documentation, READMEs, and notes. Terminal-based editors and tools frequently work with Markdown, making it a high-value tokenizer to include. ## Markdown Syntax Elements ### Headings ```markdown # Heading 1 ## Heading 2 ### Heading 3 (up to 6) Alternative Heading ================== Alternative H2 -------------- ``` ### Emphasis ```markdown *italic* or _italic_ **bold** or __bold__ ***bold italic*** ~~strikethrough~~ ``` ### Code ```markdown `inline code` indented code block (4 spaces) ```language fenced code block ``` ``` ### Links and Images ```markdown [link text](url) [link text](url "title") [reference link][ref] [ref]: url ![alt text](image.png) ![alt text](image.png "title") ``` ### Lists ```markdown - Unordered item * Also unordered + Also unordered 1. Ordered item 2. Ordered item - [ ] Task item unchecked - [x] Task item checked ``` ### Blockquotes ```markdown > Quote line > Continued quote ``` ### Horizontal Rules ```markdown --- *** ___ ``` ### Tables (GFM) ```markdown | Header | Header | |--------|--------| | Cell | Cell | ``` ### HTML ```markdown
Raw HTML
``` ## Implementation Create `src/highlight/languages/markdown.rs`: ```rust pub struct MarkdownTokenizer; impl Tokenizer for MarkdownTokenizer { fn name(&self) -> &str { "Markdown" } fn extensions(&self) -> &[&str] { &["md", "markdown", "mkd", "mkdn"] } fn tokenize_line(&self, line: &str, state: LineState) -> (Vec, LineState) { // Handle fenced code block continuation // Line-start elements: headings, lists, blockquotes, HR // Inline elements: emphasis, code, links } } ``` ### Token Mapping | Markdown Element | TokenKind | |-----------------|-----------| | `# Heading` | Heading | | `*italic*` | Emphasis | | `**bold**` | Emphasis + Bold style | | `[text](url)` | Link | | `![alt](url)` | Link | | `` `code` `` | CodeInline | | ``` ```code``` ``` | CodeBlock | | `> quote` | Comment (or custom Quote) | | `- item` | Punctuation + Text | | `---` | Punctuation | ## Multi-line Handling 1. **Fenced code blocks** - ``` ... ``` (track the fence marker) 2. **Indented code blocks** - 4+ spaces at line start 3. **Multi-line blockquotes** - Lines starting with `>` ## Edge Cases - Escaping: `\*not italic\*` - Nested emphasis: `***bold and italic***` - Links with parentheses in URL - Code spans with backticks inside: ``` `` `code` `` ``` - Setext headings (underline style) - HTML blocks ## Dependencies - Requires: bd-2x0.2 (Tokenizer trait) ## Testing ```rust #[test] fn test_md_headings() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_emphasis() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_code_inline() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_code_blocks() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_links() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_lists() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_blockquotes() { ... } #[test] fn test_md_escaping() { ... } ``` ## Files to Create/Modify - CREATE: `src/highlight/languages/markdown.rs` - MODIFY: `src/highlight/languages/mod.rs` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Headings (ATX and Setext styles) - [ ] Emphasis (italic, bold, strikethrough) - [ ] Inline and fenced code - [ ] Links and images - [ ] Lists (ordered, unordered, task) - [ ] Blockquotes - [ ] Fenced code block continuation - [ ] Escape sequences - [ ] Test coverage >85%","notes":"AGENTS.md alignment: Performance target <1μs/line. Zero-allocation tokenization. Tests must use tracing (info!, debug!) for detailed logging. Markdown is high-value for documentation editing in terminal apps.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:46:23.879677476Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T12:19:02.704966007Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T12:19:02.704947462Z","close_reason":"Implemented Markdown tokenizer + tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["highlight","lang"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2x0.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2x0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:52:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2x0.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2x0.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:52:24Z","created_by":"import"}]} -{"id":"bd-2xk1","title":"demo_showcase quality: run fmt/clippy/test gates before landing","description":"# demo_showcase Quality — Run fmt/clippy/test Gates Before Landing ## Purpose The demo will touch many parts of the codebase. This bead ensures we don’t “ship a broken flagship”. ## Required Gates Run these locally (and ensure CI passes): - `cargo fmt --check` - `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` - `cargo test` If demo modifies hot paths or rendering behavior, optionally run the buffer benchmark suite (`cargo bench --bench buffer`) as a sanity check. ## Acceptance Notes - No formatting drift. - No clippy warnings. - All tests pass, including demo compile/smoke tests.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and `cargo test` all pass after demo_showcase implementation. - demo_showcase-specific suites are included: compile coverage, headless smoke, JSON snapshots, tour determinism, degradation matrix, and core headless checks. - Optional PTY E2E tests remain `#[ignore]` but are runnable via scripts and provide actionable artifacts when enabled.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:54.666703286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:01:39.717967202Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-10de","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:50.821280848Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-1n67","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:51.409960028Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-2649","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:18.505590317Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bnv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:10.570568702Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-31gz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:52.010278354Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:54.679232573Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-3cj0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:20.439486754Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-bqd1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:19.058244445Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-jfno","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:19.654855091Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"bd-2xk1","title":"demo_showcase quality: run fmt/clippy/test gates before landing","description":"# demo_showcase Quality — Run fmt/clippy/test Gates Before Landing ## Purpose The demo will touch many parts of the codebase. This bead ensures we don’t “ship a broken flagship”. ## Required Gates Run these locally (and ensure CI passes): - `cargo fmt --check` - `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` - `cargo test` If demo modifies hot paths or rendering behavior, optionally run the buffer benchmark suite (`cargo bench --bench buffer`) as a sanity check. ## Acceptance Notes - No formatting drift. - No clippy warnings. - All tests pass, including demo compile/smoke tests.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and `cargo test` all pass after demo_showcase implementation. - demo_showcase-specific suites are included: compile coverage, headless smoke, JSON snapshots, tour determinism, degradation matrix, and core headless checks. - Optional PTY E2E tests remain `#[ignore]` but are runnable via scripts and provide actionable artifacts when enabled.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:54.666703286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:11:49.372968781Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T05:11:49.372840231Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-10de","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:50.821280848Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-1n67","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:51.409960028Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-2649","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:18.505590317Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bnv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:10.570568702Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-31gz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:52.010278354Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:54.679232573Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-3cj0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:20.439486754Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-bqd1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:19.058244445Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xk1","depends_on_id":"bd-jfno","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:19.654855091Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-313","title":"Expand benchmark coverage beyond buffer module","description":"Currently only benches/buffer.rs exists. Add benchmarks for: renderer diff detection, color blending operations, text buffer operations, unicode width calculations.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T20:28:46.184813379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T22:37:44.657047735Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T22:37:44.656970329Z","close_reason":"Comprehensive benchmarks added for color blending (creation, blending, conversion, interpolation), text buffer (TextBuffer, EditBuffer, cursor movement, undo/redo), renderer diff detection (identical, single change, row change, scattered, full diff), and unicode width calculations (ASCII, CJK, emoji, grapheme operations). All benchmarks compile and run successfully.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-31gz","title":"demo_showcase docs: README section (how to run + flags + keybinds)","description":"# demo_showcase Docs — README Section (How to Run + Flags + Keybinds) ## Purpose The flagship demo should be discoverable. Add a README section that explains: - how to run the demo - what it demonstrates - essential keybinds - tour mode and headless smoke mode - recommended terminals for best visuals ## Content Requirements Add a `Demo Showcase` section to `README.md`: Run: - cargo run --bin demo_showcase Tour (unattended): - cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --tour --exit-after-tour Headless smoke: - cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke Headless JSON dump (for tests/debugging): - cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke --headless-dump-json Degradation matrix (example manual run): - cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --headless-smoke --headless-dump-json --headless-size 50x15 --cap-preset minimal Document keybinds (or point to the in-app help overlay) and call out terminal recommendations (kitty/alacritty/wezterm/ghostty) for best results. ## Flag Summary (Must Match bd-2iv) Include a small, explicit list of supported flags (grouped by category): Interactive: - --fps - --tour - --exit-after-tour - --max-frames Headless/testing: - --headless-smoke - --headless-dump-json - --headless-check - --headless-size Terminal behavior: - --no-mouse - --no-alt-screen - --no-cap-queries Capability override: - --cap-preset Advanced: - --threaded - --seed ## Acceptance Notes - README makes it obvious the demo exists and how to run it. - Flags documented match the actual CLI (bd-2iv).","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - `README.md` contains a clear `Demo Showcase` section with run commands (interactive, tour, headless). - The documented flag list matches the implemented CLI (`bd-2iv`) including headless size + capability preset controls. - The README points users to the in-app help overlay for the authoritative keybind list.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:37.288336317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:59:51.498373446Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:59:51.498306461Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-1gy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:38.026379403Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:36.008108527Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:37.300717928Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31gz","depends_on_id":"bd-3l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:37.034595590Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-35e","title":"demo_showcase UI: toast + notification system (stack + fade)","description":"# demo_showcase UI — Toast + Notification System (Stack + Fade) ## Purpose Toasts make the demo feel like a real application and also give us a constant excuse to show: - translucent overlays (opacity stack) - animated alpha fades (time-driven) - crisp typography + spacing They also provide immediate feedback when commands/actions occur. ## Behaviors - Toasts appear in a consistent corner (e.g., bottom-right above the status bar). - Multiple toasts stack with a small vertical gap. - Each toast has: - severity: Info / Warn / Error - title + optional detail line - TTL (auto-dismiss) with fade-out - optional “action” hint (e.g., `R` retry) ## Rendering Notes - Background should be semi-transparent with a gradient edge or subtle border. - Severity affects accent color and icon glyph. - Clip the toast region with scissor so it never draws into the status bar. ## Integration Points Toasts should be produced by: - command palette executions - tour step transitions - simulated preview errors/retries - clipboard/link actions Implementation suggestion: - `struct Toast { created_at, ttl, level, title, detail, ... }` - `struct ToastManager { toasts: VecDeque }` - render in the main render pass after panels but before full-screen overlays.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - Toasts render as translucent cards with consistent spacing, typography, and severity styling. - Toasts stack with a gap and auto-dismiss after TTL with a smooth fade. - Toast region is clipped so it never draws into the status bar. - Core actions (palette commands, tour steps, paste, resize, simulated preview error) generate toasts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:23.894150944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:16:13.720258754Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:16:13.720185457Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-1pd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:00.676575823Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-2t3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:01.203719095Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-c75","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:23.906489096Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35e","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:00.094359519Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} @@ -160,7 +160,7 @@ {"id":"bd-3lio","title":"demo_showcase runtime: hard frame budget (--max-frames)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Hard Frame Budget (`--max-frames`) ## Purpose We need a deterministic, mode-independent way to bound runtime so we can: - run PTY E2E tests without hanging - run quick headless smoke checks with consistent cost - record short, repeatable demo clips This bead implements the *runtime semantics* of a hard frame budget. ## Behavior - Flag: `--max-frames ` - After the app has **presented N frames**, it exits 0. Counting rules: - Count a “frame” after a successful `present()` (or after a successful headless render+diff step). - The counter applies in **all modes**: - interactive normal - interactive tour - headless smoke - headless tour Exit contract: - Print a stable summary line to stdout: - `EXIT_OK reason=max_frames frames= last_dirty_cells=` ## Design Notes - This is intentionally separate from `--exit-after-tour`: - `--exit-after-tour` is a tour completion feature. - `--max-frames` is a generic safety bound and developer tool. ## Downstream Consumers - PTY E2E harness (`bd-61g0`) uses `--max-frames` as a hard safety bound. - Headless smoke/snapshots (`bd-2el`, `bd-1n67`, `bd-2649`, `bd-bqd1`, `bd-2bnv`) use `--max-frames` to keep runs fast and deterministic.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - `--max-frames ` exits 0 after presenting N frames in interactive mode. - `--max-frames ` also bounds headless runs consistently (smoke and tour), counting frames deterministically. - A stable exit summary line is printed (`EXIT_OK reason=max_frames ...`) and includes `last_dirty_cells`. - PTY E2E tests can use `--max-frames` as a hang-proof safety bound.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:16.243660372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:27:36.270704114Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:27:36.270561729Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3lio","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:16.259983646Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3lio","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:16.267009792Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3lio","depends_on_id":"bd-z1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:16.264016443Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-3o0","title":"demo_showcase runtime: tour mode driver (script executor + deterministic timing)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Tour Mode Driver (Script Executor + Deterministic Timing) ## Purpose Tour mode is how we get: - unattended demos (screen recordings) - deterministic behavior for tests/smoke runs - a guided narrative that proves features in a clear order This bead implements the runtime that executes the tour script defined in `bd-1gy`. ## Behaviors - CLI flag --tour starts tour immediately. - Tour runs a fixed sequence of steps: - changes focus/panels - triggers overlays (command palette/help) - scrolls lists/logs - types/pastes into editor - triggers preview animations / errors / recovery Determinism: - no randomness - timing driven by a monotonic clock + fixed durations - any synthetic inputs are generated by the tour driver Automatic termination (for recordings/E2E): - If `--exit-after-tour` is enabled (see `bd-3vjm` / `bd-2iv`), the app should exit 0 when the final tour step completes. - `--max-frames` acts as a hard safety bound; it should never be required for a normal tour, but it prevents hangs. ## Interactive + Headless The tour driver must work in BOTH: - interactive mode (real renderer) - headless mode (for CI regression tests) Headless mode should be able to execute the tour and emit JSON markers that tests can snapshot (see `bd-bqd1`). ## Implementation Approach - Introduce a TourRunner in the demo binary: - state: TourState { step_idx, step_started_at, paused, ... } - fn tick(now, app, out_actions) - The tour runner should not directly draw; it only manipulates app state and injects actions. - When the tour wants to show something, it should set a TourHighlight { rect, message } that the tour overlay bead renders. ## Acceptance Notes - cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --tour produces a stable “demo story” without user input. - Tour can be paused/resumed and cleanly exited. - Tour never leaves the app in a broken focus/input state when it ends. - With `--exit-after-tour`, the process terminates cleanly at completion.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - `cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --tour` produces a complete, deterministic feature walkthrough without user input. - Tour driving works in both interactive mode and headless mode (headless emits stable tour step markers in JSON). - Tour controls work: next/prev, pause/resume, and exit without leaving focus/input in a broken state. - With `--exit-after-tour`, the process exits 0 when the final step completes and the terminal is restored cleanly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:25:18.030533207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:47:30.666987869Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:47:30.666921105Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-1gy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:16.254927712Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:25:18.042919278Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:16.993258401Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-2t3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:18.262929423Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3o0","depends_on_id":"bd-35g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:17.664069555Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-3oqa","title":"demo_showcase runtime: event/log routing (emit_event + set_log_callback -> logs panel)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Event/Log Routing (emit_event + set_log_callback → Logs Panel) ## Purpose OpenTUI includes an event/log callback system (`src/event.rs`). The demo should use it so we: - prove the module works - get a realistic logs stream without sprinkling UI code with ad-hoc logging ## What To Implement - At demo startup: - call `set_event_callback` and `set_log_callback` - route events/logs into the demo’s internal `LogModel` (ring buffer) - Provide a small helper API in demo-only code: - `fn log_info(msg)` / `fn log_warn(msg)` / `fn log_error(msg)` - optionally `emit_event("action", json)` for structured actions ## Concurrency Notes Callbacks are `Send + Sync` and stored behind a `Mutex`. Keep callbacks fast: - push into a lock-free-ish queue (or just a bounded `VecDeque` behind a `Mutex` owned by the app) - avoid allocating large strings in hot paths ## Acceptance Notes - Core app actions (palette commands, tour steps, resize, focus changes) generate log entries. - Logs panel reflects these entries without frame hitching.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - demo_showcase installs OpenTUI event and log callbacks at startup and routes them into the app’s log model. - Core user actions (palette commands, tour steps, resize/focus changes) emit visible log entries. - Callback handlers are lightweight enough to avoid frame hitches. - The logs panel shows these entries without ad-hoc logging scattered across rendering code.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:21.888226798Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:45:53.944351497Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T04:45:53.944283160Z","close_reason":"Implemented event/log routing: set_log_callback integration, thread-safe log queue, drain_log_queue in main loop, helper functions (log_info/warn/error/debug)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oqa","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:21.908976801Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oqa","depends_on_id":"bd-2c6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:15.671602532Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3oqa","depends_on_id":"bd-hc0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:15.124481842Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} -{"id":"bd-3ot0","title":"demo_showcase runtime: optional ThreadedRenderer backend (--threaded)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Optional ThreadedRenderer Backend (`--threaded`) ## Purpose OpenTUI has a `ThreadedRenderer` that moves terminal I/O off the main thread. The flagship demo should be able to showcase this as an optional mode. This is not required for the first “works and looks amazing” milestone, but it’s an impressive proof point for real applications. ## Behavior - CLI flag: `--threaded` - When enabled, the demo uses `opentui::renderer::ThreadedRenderer` instead of `Renderer`. - The UI + state machine should behave the same. ## Design Constraint `Renderer` and `ThreadedRenderer` have different ownership models (threaded moves buffers/pools through channels). To avoid duplicating the whole app: - Introduce a small demo-only backend wrapper: - `enum Backend { Direct(Renderer), Threaded(ThreadedRenderer) }` - helper methods: `buffer()`, `present()`, `resize()`, `set_title()`, `set_cursor()` ## GraphemePool Note (Important) If tour/unicode panels use grapheme pool content, ensure that the threaded path does not leak grapheme refs: - Avoid using `ThreadedRenderer::clear()` if it doesn’t clear with pool. - Prefer explicit `buffer().clear_with_pool(grapheme_pool(), bg)` / `resize_with_pool` patterns. - If the library needs a small fix (pool-aware clear/resize convenience), file a follow-up bead or patch the library directly. ## Acceptance Notes - `cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --threaded` works and exits cleanly. - Rendering remains smooth and input remains responsive.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - `cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --threaded` runs the full app using `ThreadedRenderer` and behaves like the direct backend. - Input remains responsive and rendering remains smooth. - Grapheme pool usage is safe in the threaded path (no leaked refs; correct pool-aware clear/resize semantics). - The app exits cleanly and restores terminal state.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:45.107585716Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:55:48.778955923Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","features","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:45.142106801Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:18.844522494Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:19.962802535Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-z1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:19.396568255Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"bd-3ot0","title":"demo_showcase runtime: optional ThreadedRenderer backend (--threaded)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Optional ThreadedRenderer Backend (`--threaded`) ## Purpose OpenTUI has a `ThreadedRenderer` that moves terminal I/O off the main thread. The flagship demo should be able to showcase this as an optional mode. This is not required for the first “works and looks amazing” milestone, but it’s an impressive proof point for real applications. ## Behavior - CLI flag: `--threaded` - When enabled, the demo uses `opentui::renderer::ThreadedRenderer` instead of `Renderer`. - The UI + state machine should behave the same. ## Design Constraint `Renderer` and `ThreadedRenderer` have different ownership models (threaded moves buffers/pools through channels). To avoid duplicating the whole app: - Introduce a small demo-only backend wrapper: - `enum Backend { Direct(Renderer), Threaded(ThreadedRenderer) }` - helper methods: `buffer()`, `present()`, `resize()`, `set_title()`, `set_cursor()` ## GraphemePool Note (Important) If tour/unicode panels use grapheme pool content, ensure that the threaded path does not leak grapheme refs: - Avoid using `ThreadedRenderer::clear()` if it doesn’t clear with pool. - Prefer explicit `buffer().clear_with_pool(grapheme_pool(), bg)` / `resize_with_pool` patterns. - If the library needs a small fix (pool-aware clear/resize convenience), file a follow-up bead or patch the library directly. ## Acceptance Notes - `cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --threaded` works and exits cleanly. - Rendering remains smooth and input remains responsive.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - `cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- --threaded` runs the full app using `ThreadedRenderer` and behaves like the direct backend. - Input remains responsive and rendering remains smooth. - Grapheme pool usage is safe in the threaded path (no leaked refs; correct pool-aware clear/resize semantics). - The app exits cleanly and restores terminal state.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:45.107585716Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:12:10.796221198Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T05:12:10.796153562Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","features","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:26:45.142106801Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:18.844522494Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:19.962802535Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ot0","depends_on_id":"bd-z1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:19.396568255Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-3pe","title":"demo_showcase UI: help overlay (F1) + feature legend","description":"# demo_showcase UI — Help Overlay (F1) + Feature Legend ## Purpose The help overlay makes the demo self-contained and also proves multiple library features: - overlay rendering + opacity stack (glass modal) - scissor clipping (scrollable help content) - hyperlink rendering via OSC 8 (LinkPool) It also prevents the “what do I press?” problem for first-time viewers. ## Content Requirements ### Keybinds Include (at minimum): - `Ctrl+Q` quit - `Tab` / `Shift+Tab` focus next/prev panel - `Ctrl+P` command palette - `F1` help - `Ctrl+T` tour start/stop - editor keys (arrows, word movement, wrap toggle, undo/redo) - log/preview navigation keys ### Mouse - click to focus panels - click to activate buttons - scroll to scroll lists/logs ### Feature Legend Make it explicit where each OpenTUI feature is visible in the app: - RGBA alpha blending (preview overlays / glass modals) - scissor stack (sidebar/log scroll clipping) - opacity stack (overlays + tinted UI sections) - grapheme pool (unicode panel) - OSC 8 links (logs + help) - hitgrid (clickable UI) - pixel buffers (preview panel) - diff rendering + sync output (perf/inspector + smoothness) ### Clickable Links Include a small “Docs / Links” section with OSC 8 hyperlinks: - repo URL - unicode width reference The links must be allocated through `renderer.link_pool()` and applied via `Style::with_link`. ## Rendering Notes - Help content should be scrollable when terminal is small. - Use clear typography hierarchy: title, section headers, monospace keycaps. - Use hit areas for the “Close” button and for any tab-like subviews.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - F1 toggles a help overlay that clearly explains keybinds, mouse interactions, and where each OpenTUI feature is demonstrated. - Help content is scrollable and clipped via scissor when space is limited. - Help overlay includes OSC 8 hyperlinks (when supported) allocated via `renderer.link_pool()`. - Close controls work via both keyboard and HitGrid.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:04.642163943Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:39:33.626537097Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:39:33.626442561Z","close_reason":"Help overlay expanded: Mouse section, Feature Legend (maps all OpenTUI features to visible locations), Links section. Scrollable content with glass panel styling.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3pe","depends_on_id":"bd-1al","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:04.565909821Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pe","depends_on_id":"bd-1ei","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:05.666002312Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pe","depends_on_id":"bd-3l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:05.092958035Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3pe","depends_on_id":"bd-c75","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:04.656207314Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-3vjm","title":"demo_showcase runtime: auto-exit on tour completion (--exit-after-tour)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Auto‑Exit on Tour Completion (`--exit-after-tour`) ## Purpose We need a deterministic way for an unattended tour run to terminate cleanly so we can: - record demos reliably (tour ends -> app exits) - run PTY E2E tests without brittle "send keys" logic This bead implements the runtime semantics of exiting when the tour completes. ## Behavior - Flag: `--exit-after-tour` - When tour mode reaches the final step and completes it, the process exits 0 automatically. Exit must be clean: - restore cursor - leave alt screen (if used) - disable mouse (if enabled) This flag does **not** change interactive controls (Esc still exits tour early). ## Relationship to `--max-frames` - `--max-frames` is a generic hard frame budget used as a safety bound (tracked separately in `bd-3lio`). - PTY tests should typically run with BOTH: - `--exit-after-tour` (intended terminator) - `--max-frames` (hang-proof safety bound) ## Notes - Interactive users should not be surprised: - Only enable this flag when explicitly requested. - Consider showing a small status hint (e.g., “auto-exit after tour”) so it’s discoverable.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - `--exit-after-tour` exits 0 when the final tour step completes (interactive + PTY runs). - Exit is clean: cursor restored, mouse disabled (if enabled), and alt screen exited (if used). - The tour remains user-controllable: Esc can still exit tour early and does not force process exit. - PTY E2E tests can rely on this to terminate without injecting synthetic quit keys.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:07.678047128Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:41:59.311470465Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:41:59.311402579Z","close_reason":"Verified --exit-after-tour implementation: flag parsing, TourRunner exit_on_complete tracking, and App.should_quit/exit_reason setting on tour completion. Added test_exit_after_tour test to verify functionality.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vjm","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:07.707235213Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vjm","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:07.718517533Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vjm","depends_on_id":"bd-3o0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:07.723946582Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vjm","depends_on_id":"bd-z1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:07.712843797Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"bd-61g0","title":"demo_showcase e2e: PTY harness + ANSI capture (real terminal sequences, ignored by default)","description":"# demo_showcase E2E — PTY Harness + ANSI Capture (Ignored by Default) ## Purpose Headless tests prove the render pipeline is correct, but they cannot prove real terminal I/O behavior. This bead adds true end-to-end tests by running the demo under a pseudo-terminal (PTY), capturing ANSI output bytes, and asserting on key invariants: - alternate screen entry/exit - cursor hide/show - mouse enable sequences (if enabled) - synchronized output enable (if supported) - OSC 8 hyperlink sequences appear (and do NOT appear when disabled) ## Environment Normalization (Critical for Determinism) Capability detection in OpenTUI is environment-driven. PTY runs often have sparse env. To make E2E behavior deterministic, the PTY harness MUST set a known-good env baseline for the child process, e.g.: - `TERM=xterm-kitty` (or another known terminal string) - `TERM_PROGRAM=kitty` (optional if TERM-based detection is implemented, but keep for redundancy) - `COLORTERM=truecolor` - `LANG=C.UTF-8` This ensures: - `Capabilities::detect()` can enable sync output + hyperlinks on a "known" terminal (see `bd-1xsw`). ## Approach (Unix) Use libc openpty / fork+exec (or equivalent) to spawn the demo under a PTY and capture output. Recommended primary case: - cargo run --all-features --bin demo_showcase -- --tour --exit-after-tour --max-frames 600 --fps 30 Why both exit controls? - `--exit-after-tour` is the intended deterministic terminator. - `--max-frames` is a hard safety bound so the PTY test cannot hang if tour logic regresses. ## Assertions (Positive) Assert the output stream contains, in some order: - enter alt screen (CSI ? 1049 h) when alt screen enabled - leave alt screen (CSI ? 1049 l) on exit - hide cursor (CSI ? 25 l) and show cursor (CSI ? 25 h) - mouse enable sequences (1000/1002/1006) when mouse enabled - synchronized output enable (CSI ? 2026 h) when supported/enabled Hyperlink coverage: - run one case with default settings and assert at least one OSC 8 sequence appears (ESC ] 8 ; ; ...) - run one case with `--cap-preset no_hyperlinks` (disable) and assert OSC 8 sequences are absent ## Assertions (Negative / Flag Coverage) Add explicit "feature disabled" cases so the CLI flags are verifiably correct: - `--no-alt-screen`: assert alt-screen enter/exit sequences are absent - `--no-mouse`: assert mouse enable sequences are absent (Other invariants like cursor restore should still hold.) ## Optional (High-Value): Threaded Mode Parity - run an additional spawn with `--threaded` and assert the same terminal lifecycle invariants ## Logging / Artifacts When the test runs, log (to stdout/stderr, and to artifacts when enabled): - full spawn command (argv) - exit status + duration - bytes captured - TERM/TERM_PROGRAM/COLORTERM/LANG used for the child (and any env overrides) Artifacts (controlled via HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1): - captured ANSI bytes (raw) - escaped/hex view for grep-friendly debugging - stdout/stderr from the child process Reuse the existing harness conventions: - HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1 - HARNESS_ARTIFACTS_DIR=target/test-artifacts ## Test Placement - Mark PTY tests as #[ignore] by default to avoid CI flakiness. - Provide a script (separate bead) to run them locally and collect artifacts.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when: - An ignored PTY-based test suite can spawn `demo_showcase` with `--tour --exit-after-tour --max-frames` and capture a non-trivial ANSI output stream. - The PTY harness normalizes the child env (TERM/TERM_PROGRAM/COLORTERM/LANG) so capability detection is deterministic. - Assertions cover: alt screen enter/exit, cursor hide/show, mouse enable (when enabled), and sync output enable (when supported/enabled). - Hyperlink behavior is covered: OSC 8 sequences present by default and absent under `--cap-preset no_hyperlinks`. - Flag coverage includes negative cases: - `--no-alt-screen` -> no 1049 enter/exit sequences - `--no-mouse` -> no mouse enable sequences - Optional: a threaded run case (`--threaded`) also passes the same terminal lifecycle assertions. - On failure, artifacts include raw and escaped/hex output plus env+argv logging when `HARNESS_ARTIFACTS=1`.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:04.613268330Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:54:54.715128030Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:54:54.715033144Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-1ob","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:23:05.470334996Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-1xsw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:32:17.315779764Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:27.862668126Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-383","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:04.631306455Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-3lio","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:29:07.021451096Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-3o0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:28.902157577Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-3vjm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:19.691230196Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-61g0","depends_on_id":"bd-z1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:26.694765952Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
